title: Project Report: ITSM Incident Management Setup — ServiceNow Developer / Administrator
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Configure incident table, assignment groups, SLAs, and email-to-incident on Personal Developer Instance.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**ITSM Incident Management Setup** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for ServiceNow Developer / Administrator. Duration: 2–3 weeks. Configure incident table, assignment groups, SLAs, and email-to-incident on Personal Developer Instance. Tools: ServiceNow PDI, ITSM module.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Configure assignment groups
Objective: Set SLA definitions
Objective: Email-to-incident inbound action
Objective: Create 5 test incidents
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
ServiceNow Developer / Administrator portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a ServiceNow Developer / Administrator resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a ServiceNow Developer / Administrator resume with a demo link.

chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Groups** *(Level: Beginner)*

Assignment groups + roles. Complete these tasks: Service Desk; Network Team. Deliverable for this phase: Group structure.

Code Reference:

```text
- [ ] Network Team
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: PDI config export; SLA doc; Test screenshots. Skills demonstrated: ITSM, SLA, Configuration. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: PDI config export
Deliverable: SLA doc
Deliverable: Test screenshots
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits PDI config export and passes the rubric.
